content: Can you describe the main types of tasks you do in your job?
I am communicating with customers regardingvpurshases they done

When you’re communicating with customers about the purchases they’ve done, do you ever use generative AI to help (like drafting replies or explaining issues), and can you walk me through one recent example?
Yes K fobodtrn draft mh letters with chat gpt
When you use ChatGPT to draft your letters to customers, what do you usually change or double-check before you send them?
I make s core nessage and let chat makd ut polite and smooth

summary:
## Rolling interview summary (so far)

### Role / main tasks
- Interviewee’s job involves **communicating with customers about purchases they have made** (customer correspondence).

### GenAI usage & perceived usefulness (Prompt 1)
- **Uses GenAI:** Yes—uses **ChatGPT** to **draft letters/messages to customers**.
- **How they use it (workflow):**
  - They first create the **core message/content** themselves.
  - Then they use ChatGPT to **rewrite it to be more polite and smooth** (tone/wording refinement).
- **Perceived usefulness:**
  - ChatGPT is useful for **polishing customer communications**, especially improving **politeness and fluency**.
- **Examples provided:**
  - General example: “draft my letters with ChatGPT” (no specific incident or outcome details yet).
- **Non-usage / avoidance / harms:**
  - Not discussed yet.

### Checks / quality control
- They **double-check by ensuring the core message is correct**, then rely on ChatGPT mainly for **style/tone** rather than substance.

### Emotions, ease of use, risks, human-likeness, future intentions
- Not covered yet in the conversation so far; no notable emotional discomfort expressed.
content: When you start from your core message and use ChatGPT to make it more polite and smooth, how easy or tricky is it to get the tone and wording the way you want?

summary:
## Rolling interview summary (updated)

### Role / main tasks
- Job involves **communicating with customers about purchases they have made** (customer correspondence).

### GenAI usage & perceived usefulness (Prompt 1)
- **Uses GenAI:** Yes—uses **ChatGPT** to **draft/rewrite letters/messages to customers**.
- **How they use it (workflow):**
  - Writes the **core message/content** themselves first.
  - Uses ChatGPT to **rewrite/polish** for **politeness, smoothness, and professionalism** (tone/wording refinement rather than substance).
- **Perceived usefulness:**
  - Particularly useful for **making communication sound professional** and appropriately toned.
- **Concrete examples:**
  - When they **don’t know a customer well**, ChatGPT tends to produce a **neutral tone**, which they find suitable.
  - For **long-time/older customers**, they sometimes need to **personalize** the email and will **prompt ChatGPT to be more casual and friendly**.
- **Unsuccessful use case:**
  - They report that **sometimes the attempt to make the message more casual/friendly “does not work”** (no specific failure instance described yet).

### Perceived ease of use & integration (Prompt 2)
- Describes getting the desired polite/smooth tone as **“quite easy.”**
- They created a reusable **saved “project” prompt/setup** and **reuse it** for customer communication tasks.
- **Friction noted:** While generally easy, they sometimes **can’t get the desired casual/friendly personalization** even when prompting.

### Emotional drivers / feelings around GenAI (Prompt 3)
- Using the saved project prompt makes them feel **more confident**, specifically because it helps them feel they are **communicating professionally**.
- **Sensitivity / watch-outs for follow-up:** Mild frustration implied when ChatGPT **doesn’t achieve the requested casual/friendly tone**; emotional impact not fully explored yet (no detailed episode or aftermath described).

### Perceived tech characteristics, human-likeness/agency, risks & future intentions (Prompts 5–7)
- Not covered yet.
content: When you notice it doesn’t quite nail the tone—like when you want it more casual and friendly—what patterns have you started to see in when it works well versus when it doesn’t?

summary:
## Rolling interview summary (updated)

### Role / main tasks
- Job involves **communicating with customers about purchases they have made** (customer correspondence).

### GenAI usage & perceived usefulness (Prompt 1)
- **Uses GenAI:** Yes—uses **ChatGPT** to **draft/rewrite letters/messages to customers**.
- **How they use it (workflow):**
  - Writes the **core message/content** themselves first.
  - Uses ChatGPT to **rewrite/polish** for **politeness, smoothness, and professionalism** (tone/wording refinement rather than substance).
- **Perceived usefulness:**
  - Particularly useful for making communication sound **professional** and appropriately toned.
- **Concrete examples:**
  - When they **don’t know a customer well**, ChatGPT’s **neutral tone** is seen as suitable.
  - For **long-time/older customers**, they sometimes need to **personalize** the email and will **prompt ChatGPT to be more casual and friendly**.
- **Unsuccessful / weaker use cases:**
  - Sometimes the attempt to make the message **more casual/friendly doesn’t work**.
  - Sometimes ChatGPT uses **words/phrases they would never use**, so the message **doesn’t sound like “me.”**
  - When this happens, they may ask it to **correct spelling** (as a follow-up adjustment).

### Perceived ease of use & integration (Prompt 2)
- Getting the desired **polite/smooth** tone is described as **“quite easy.”**
- They created a reusable **saved “project” prompt/setup** and **reuse it** for customer communication tasks.
- **Friction noted:** Difficulty getting the desired **casual/friendly personalization**; also occasional mismatch with their personal wording/voice.

### Emotional drivers / feelings around GenAI (Prompt 3)
- The saved project prompt makes them feel **more confident**, because it helps ensure they are **communicating professionally**.
- **Sensitivity / watch-outs for follow-up:** Mild frustration implied when outputs don’t match their voice or desired friendliness; emotional impact not yet explored in detail.

### Perceived tech characteristics: reliability, consistency, fit (Prompt 5)
- **Perceived inconsistency by time of day:** They report ChatGPT generally works **worse after ~2pm**, which they attribute to **higher US usage/traffic** (“market is up and a lot of people are using it”).
- **Perceived reliability issue:** Output quality can vary (e.g., more “off” wording/phrases) depending on when they use it.

### Perceived human-likeness/agency, risks & future intentions (Prompts 6–7)
- Not covered yet.
content: When ChatGPT starts sounding “less like you” or feels a bit off—especially later in the day—what does it make you think is going on on its side?

### Emotional drivers / feelings around GenAI (Prompt 3)
- The saved project prompt makes them feel **more confident**, because it helps ensure they are **communicating professionally**.
- **Sensitivity / watch-outs for follow-up:** Mild frustration implied when outputs don’t match their voice or desired friendliness; emotional impact not yet explored in detail.

### Perceived tech characteristics: reliability, consistency, fit (Prompt 5)
- **Perceived inconsistency by time of day:** They report ChatGPT generally works **worse after ~2pm**, which they attribute to **higher US usage/traffic** (“market is up and a lot of people are using it”).

summary:
## Rolling interview summary (updated)

### Role / main tasks
- Job involves **communicating with customers about purchases they have made** (customer correspondence).

### GenAI usage & perceived usefulness (Prompt 1)
- **Uses GenAI:** Yes—uses **ChatGPT** to **draft/rewrite letters/messages to customers**.
- **How they use it (workflow):**
  - Writes the **core message/content** themselves first.
  - Uses ChatGPT to **rewrite/polish** for **politeness, smoothness, and professionalism** (tone/wording refinement rather than substance).
- **Perceived usefulness:**
  - Particularly useful for making communication sound **professional** and appropriately toned.
- **Concrete examples:**
  - When they **don’t know a customer well**, ChatGPT’s **neutral tone** is seen as suitable.
  - For **long-time/older customers**, they sometimes need to **personalize** the email and will **prompt ChatGPT to be more casual and friendly**.
- **Unsuccessful / weaker use cases:**
  - Sometimes the attempt to make the message **more casual/friendly doesn’t work**.
  - Sometimes ChatGPT uses **words/phrases they would never use**, so the message **doesn’t sound like “me.”**
  - When this happens, they may ask it to **correct spelling** (as a follow-up adjustment).

### Perceived ease of use & integration (Prompt 2)
- Getting the desired **polite/smooth** tone is described as **“quite easy.”**
- They created a reusable **saved “project” prompt/setup** and **reuse it** for customer communication tasks.
- **Friction noted:** Difficulty getting the desired **casual/friendly personalization**; occasional mismatch with their personal wording/voice.

### Emotional drivers / feelings around GenAI (Prompt 3)
- The saved project prompt makes them feel **more confident**, because it helps ensure they are **communicating professionally**.
- **Stronger negative emotion surfaced:** When ChatGPT output quality feels “off” (especially later in the day), they find it **“quite irritating.”**
  - **Behavioral impact:** If they have **complicated tasks** and need ChatGPT’s help, they may **postpone** those tasks until later/another time.

### Perceived tech characteristics: reliability, consistency, fit (Prompt 5)
- **Perceived inconsistency by time of day:** They report ChatGPT generally works **worse after ~2pm**, which they attribute to **higher US usage/traffic** (“market is up and a lot of people are using it”).
- **Hypothesized cause:** They **guess** OpenAI may **switch to another model** when usage increases.
- **Perceived reliability issue:** Output quality can vary (e.g., more “off” wording/phrases) depending on when they use it.

### Perceived human-likeness and agency (Prompt 6)
- They do **not** describe ChatGPT as “choosing” to be unhelpful; instead they attribute issues to **system/usage factors** (traffic/load) and possibly **model switching** (i.e., more like a tool affected by conditions than an agent with intent).

### Risks, barriers, future intentions (Prompt 7)
- Not covered yet.

### Sensitivity / watch-outs for follow-up
- **Irritation/frustration** is notable when ChatGPT performance drops; it can **disrupt workflow** (task postponement). Potential area to probe: how this affects **trust**, willingness to use for **high-stakes** messages, and any coping strategies (fallback tools/processes).
content: Thinking about those moments when you feel you have to postpone work because ChatGPT isn’t reliable, what’s the biggest thing that makes you hesitate to use it more widely in your day-to-day tasks?
